hero:
# Government Employee Salary Analysis

**Public-sector compensation EDA & interactive dashboard · Python · Pandas · Plotly Dash**

## Hero Overview

An analysis of Louisville Metro government employee compensation across 2015–2023, pulling live salary records straight from the city's open ArcGIS REST API and profiling pay structure by department, job title, and year.

Findings are packaged into an interactive Plotly Dash dashboard where users filter by department and compose custom salary visualizations on the fly.

points.evidence: Pulled Louisville Metro salary records (2015–2023) directly from the city's open ArcGIS REST API, handling year filters and offset pagination with requests and pandas.json_normalize.
Profiled compensation across departments, job titles, and years with per-employee trend lines, correlation heatmaps, distribution pies, and rate-vs-total scatter plots (Matplotlib, Seaborn, Plotly).
Built a Plotly Dash app with a department selector and a build-your-own-chart panel (choose X/Y axes, histogram vs. scatter) over label-encoded categorical fields.

network_graph.title: Government Salary Analysis Network
network_graph.description: API-driven ingestion, salary EDA, and an interactive department dashboard.

storyline:
# Storyline

## Context

Government pay data is public but scattered behind APIs and rarely examined as a time series; raw salary tables obscure how compensation actually shifts across departments, roles, and years.

## Workflow

Live API ingestion & pagination → cleaning and feature engineering → multi-year exploratory analysis → an interactive department dashboard.

## Technical Implementation

- **Ingestion:** pulled Louisville Metro salary records (2015–2023) directly from the city's open ArcGIS REST API, handling year filters and offset pagination with `requests` + `pandas.json_normalize`.
- **Cleaning & features:** column normalization, null handling, per-employee IDs via `groupby`, and a derived `Calculated_Total` (regular + incentive + overtime).
- **EDA:** per-employee salary trends, year-over-year mean shifts, department distribution pies, correlation heatmaps, and rate-vs-total scatter plots with Matplotlib, Seaborn, and Plotly.
- **Dashboard:** a Plotly Dash app with a department selector plus a build-your-own-chart panel (choose X/Y axes and histogram vs. scatter) over label-encoded categorical fields.

## Outcomes

Turned a raw public-sector salary API into a reproducible pipeline and an interactive dashboard, surfacing department-level pay structure and multi-year compensation trends for Louisville Metro.
